title: Trump threatens, praises and reassures allies at NATO summit
subhead: In Turkey, the American president, after criticizing Spain and reiterating his ambitions regarding Greenland, reaffirmed his commitment to the alliance's mutual defense clause and gave Ukraine the green light to produce Patriot missiles under license.

title: Renewed US-Iran hostilities highlight Tehran's deep mistrust of negotiations
Le Pen cuts short first presidential campaign trip amid protests
Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for US Senate, exits race after rape allegation
subhead: Platner, the Democratic Senate nominee for Maine and the party's hope to flip a Republican-held seat, ended his campaign following a rape allegation. He has denied the accusations.

title: EWC 2026 in Paris: Behind the scenes of the controversial partnership between France and Saudi Arabia in esports
subhead: By hosting this Saudi video game tournament in Paris, with an opening ceremony on Wednesday, July 8, featuring a concert by Aya Nakamura, the Elysée is endorsing the kingdom's dominance in the sector – but not without facing criticism from the French esports community.

title: France vs. Morocco: The Atlas Lions seek redemption four years after their World Cup semifinal defeat in Qatar
subhead: The Moroccan national football team has only beaten France once, during a friendly tournament in May 1998. 28 years later, Moroccan supporters have reason to believe their team, stronger than ever, can claim victory in the 2026 World Cup quarterfinals.

title: How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

Macron expresses 'belief that a new era is dawning in Syria' in Damascus visit
Marine Le Pen, phoenix of French far right, embarks on Elysée race
subhead: Marine Le Pen announced she would run for president hours after her embezzlement conviction was upheld.

title: Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
subhead: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

title: Philosopher Mélanie Plouviez: 'The rich are not rich alone; they are in a social world that protects them'
subhead: Wealth is not determined solely by the possessions and assets one accumulates, philosopher Mélanie Plouviez explains in an interview with Le Monde. Since antiquity, a person's fortune has also been defined by one's social standing, relationships of dominance, dependence and honor.

title: Marine Le Pen's shifting principles
'A World Cup is not a reflection of international relations, or if it is, it is a particularly distorted one'
'Diverse France, that of Kylian Mbappé and the national team, is too promising a reality to be used as an electoral punching bag'
